Rod of Mourning  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 2H Blunt Atk Delay: 36
DMG: 29 Dmg Bonus: 132
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +5 WIS: +5 INT: +5 AGI: -5 HP: +25 MANA: +25 ENDUR: +25
Effect: Force (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 50
WT: 8.5 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD MNK BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

#Anonymous, Posted: May 31 2001 at 3:09 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Bad monkey weapon too much wieght.
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 05 2001 at 2:30 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) if I were a monk I don't think i'd EVER use a 2hb... accept for a few special ones who's name I can't remember.. I believe one had dmg/dly of 44/40... now that I would use. other then that... you get dual weild.. why defeat the purpose of that unless you were fighting a mob with a dmg shield?
Never use this?
QuoteReply
# Oct 29 2002 at 11:35 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759503792
74 posts
One good time to use a weapon like this is on mobs with damage shields. I always use 2hs on spiders in DL.
▲ Page top
RE: Monks
QuoteReply
# Feb 05 2002 at 1:08 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758661243
48 posts
I thought the same thing, was dual wielding a Trance stick and a SoS..had a guy in EC offer to trade me a IFS 38/40 2hb for the SoS. My skill sucked in 2hb but i made the trade based on cash difference in the 2 items. Well 4 days later i had my 2hb maxed and havent dual wielded since.
At 52 with max skill and a str buff my best round to date has been 205, 196, and a kick for 111. Thats more then 500hp in 1 round...no way i was coming close to that including the proc when dual weilding. Another thing i like about using 2hb is it is alot easier for a tank to grab taunt off when a mob is pulled, and i dont grab agro anymore and waist our clerics mana during battles.
At the very least it is a skill to try and keep current.as to be able to test it out for yerself :)
▲ Page top
RE: Monks
QuoteReply
# Sep 17 2001 at 9:16 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591761128024
Scholar
66 posts
Aggro is funny in some ways. There is a time factor to it, meaning that if you hit a mob often you will get more aggro. Therefore, if you hit a mob for the same amount of damage with two one hand blunt weapons as with a two hand blunt you will get more aggro when dual wielding. This may not be an issue in every day life but when fighting in higher level dungeons you really want to make sure that the main tank is keeping the aggro. Sure, you can take hits there too but it's really a waste of the clerics mana supply and unnecessary. When fighting mobs of level 65 and such it can be imperative to have one person taking hits and several clerics chain healing him. As an sk I carry shield and one hand blunt with me for when I need to tank and a two hand blunt for when I just need to do damage.
▲ Page top
RE: Monks

# May 14 2001 at 3:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754223706
2 posts
As Paladins and Clerics share some of the same spells: It is both a Level34 Cleric spell, and a Level 52 Paladin Spell. It does a Max. of 90 Damage and Stuns for up to 6 seconds.



Opius Divine, 58th Paladin, The Seventh Hammer.
